2606.27667.title: Explainable AI for Biodiversity Monitoring and Ecological Image Analysis
2606.27667.paper_summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming biodiversity monitoring by enabling automated analysis of ecological imagery collected from camera traps, drones, satellites, underwater platforms, and other sensing systems. These tools can expand the scale and speed of conservation assessments, yet many computer vision models remain difficult to inspect, making it challenging to determine whether predictions are based on ecologically meaningful signals or on spurious correlations, sampling biases, and other artifacts that may undermine conservation decisions. We argue that explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) should become a standard component of ecological model validation because conservation practitioners increasingly depend on understanding not only whether a model is accurate, but why it is accurate. We provide practical guidance for applying XAI to three common ecological computer vision tasks: image classification, object detection, and image segmentation. To illustrate how XAI can support ecological model auditing, refinement, and deployment, we present two case studies using aerial imagery: harbor seal detection and cetacean anatomical segmentation. These examples demonstrate how explanation methods can identify biologically meaningful cues, reveal false positives driven by background and shape confounds, uncover edge and occlusion effects, and guide data collection, augmentation, and retraining strategies. More broadly, they show how explainability can help assess whether model reasoning aligns with ecological understanding. We conclude by identifying key challenges and opportunities. By making model behavior more transparent and scientifically interrogable, XAI can help ensure that AI-supported ecological evidence is more reliable, understandable, and actionable for biodiversity conservation.
2606.27667.paper_summary_zh: 人工智慧正在改變生物多樣性監測，通過自動分析從攝影陷阱、無人機、衛星、水下平台和其他感測系統收集的生態影像。這些工具可以擴大保育評估的規模和速度，但許多計算機視覺模型仍然難以檢查，這使得確定預測是否基於生態上有意義的信號或基於虛假的相關性、取樣偏差及其他可能削弱保育決策的工件變得具有挑戰性。我們主張可解釋的人工智慧（XAI）應成為生態模型驗證的標準組成部分，因為保育從業者越來越依賴於理解模型不僅是否準確，還有為什麼準確。我們提供了將XAI應用於三個常見生態計算機視覺任務的實用指導：影像分類、物體檢測和影像分割。為了說明XAI如何支持生態模型的審核、改進和部署，我們展示了兩個使用空中影像的案例研究：港口海豹檢測和鯨類解剖分割。這些例子展示了解釋方法如何識別生物學上有意義的線索，揭示由背景和形狀混淆驅動的假陽性，發現邊緣和遮擋效應，並指導數據收集、增強和再訓練策略。更廣泛地說，它們展示了可解釋性如何幫助評估模型推理是否與生態理解相符。我們最後指出了關鍵挑戰和機會。通過使模型行為更加透明和科學可質疑，XAI可以幫助確保AI支持的生態證據對生物多樣性保護更加可靠、可理解和可行。
2606.27667.author: Brinnae Bent et.al.
2606.27667.authors: Brinnae Bent, Holly R. Houliston, Jiayi Zhou, Günel Aghakishiyeva, David W. Johnston
